Sync beads JSONL with latest concurrent agent changes.

 {"id":"bd-1nl75.7.2","title":"G.2: Benchmark — measure I/O reduction with strategies","description":"## G.2: Benchmark — Measure I/O Reduction

### What To Build

A criterion benchmark that measures tick count reduction for each strategy compared to the baseline (all screens ticked every frame).

### Benchmark Setup

- Mock model with 15 screens (matching am's screen count)
- Each "tick" records an atomic counter increment
- Run 1000 frames with each strategy
- Measure total ticks across all screens

### Expected Results

| Strategy           | Total Ticks (1000 frames) | Reduction vs Baseline |
|--------------------|--------------------------|----------------------|
| None (baseline)    | 15,000                   | 0%                   |
| ActiveOnly         | 1,000                    | 93%                  |
| Uniform(5)         | 1,000 + 14×200 = 3,800   | 75%                  |
| ActivePlusAdjacent | 1,000 + 2×1000 + 12×200 = 5,400 | 64%        |
| Predictive (warm)  | ~2,500-4,000 (depends on distribution) | 73-83% |

### What This Validates

- Strategies produce measurably fewer ticks than baseline
- Predictive concentrates ticks on high-probability screens
- No strategy accidentally blocks or starves screens

### Dependencies

- Depends on all strategy implementations (C.2-C.5)
- Depends on D.2 (tick dispatch uses strategy)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T02:32:02.907696657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T02:33:15.917282378Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","perf","testing","tick-strategy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T02:33:14.257511044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T02:33:14.669707455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T02:33:15.091146935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T02:33:15.497111993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T02:33:15.917225762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T02:32:02.907696657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1nl75.7.3","title":"G.3: Property tests — Markov chain invariants","description":"## G.3: Property Tests — Markov Chain Invariants

### What To Build

Property-based tests (proptest/quickcheck) that verify mathematical invariants of the Markov chain and probability system.

### Invariants to Test

1. **Probability normalization**: For any screen, sum of all outgoing probabilities = 1.0 (within floating-point epsilon)

2. **Monotonicity**: If count(A→B) > count(A→C), then probability(A→B) > probability(A→C)

3. **Divisor monotonicity**: Higher probability → lower divisor (more frequent ticking)

4. **Decay idempotency**: decay(1.0) is identity (no change)

5. **Decay monotonicity**: decay(0.5) produces strictly smaller counts than decay(0.9) on the same data

6. **Merge commutativity**: merge(A, B) produces same result as merge(B, A)

7. **Merge associativity**: merge(merge(A, B), C) = merge(A, merge(B, C))

8. **Round-trip serialization**: save(load(save(counter))) = save(counter)

9. **Confidence bounds**: 0.0 <= confidence <= 1.0 for any input

10. **Divisor bounds**: min_divisor <= divisor_for(p) <= max_divisor for any 0 <= p <= 1

### Property Test Strategy

Use proptest with:
- Random screen IDs (strings of length 1-20)
- Random transition sequences (1-1000 transitions)
- Random decay factors (0.01-0.99)
- Random probability values (0.0-1.0)

### Dependencies

- Depends on A.1 (TransitionCounter)
- Depends on B.1 (MarkovPredictor)
- Depends on B.2 (TickAllocation)
- Depends on E.1 (serialization)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T02:32:17.689485339Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T02:33:17.576662731Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["perf","testing","tick-strategy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T02:33:16.331153083Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T02:33:16.743112650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T02:33:17.163230948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T02:33:17.576572392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T02:32:17.689485339Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1nl75.8","title":"Track H: Observability, Tracing, and Configuration","description":"## Track H: Observability, Tracing, and Configuration

### Purpose

Make the tick strategy system fully observable and configurable. Without this, debugging tick issues requires reading source code. With this, operators can see exactly what the strategy is doing via tracing logs, debug screens, and env vars.

### Why This Is Essential

The original problem (TUI flashing in mcp-agent-mail) took extensive investigation to diagnose. With proper observability baked into the framework, any future tick-related issue would be immediately visible: "screen X is ticking at divisor 1 when it should be at divisor 10" appears in tracing output, not buried in silent code paths.

### Subtasks

- H.1: Structured tracing for tick strategy decisions
- H.2: Debug stats surface for runtime introspection
- H.3: Environment variable configuration for mcp-agent-mail","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:16:41.011675351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T03:16:41.011675351Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["observability","perf","tick-strategy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:16:41.011675351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1nl75.8.1","title":"H.1: Structured tracing for tick strategy decisions","description":"## H.1: Structured Tracing for Tick Strategy Decisions

### What To Build

Add tracing spans and events to the tick strategy system so operators can see exactly what the strategy is doing via RUST_LOG or similar.

### Tracing Points

1. **Strategy initialization** (INFO):
   \`tick_strategy.init: name=\"Predictive\" fallback_divisor=5 min_observations=20\`

2. **Screen transition detected** (DEBUG):
   \`tick_strategy.transition: from=\"Dashboard\" to=\"Messages\" total_transitions=247\`

3. **Divisor cache refresh** (DEBUG):
   \`tick_strategy.cache_refresh: active=\"Messages\" screens=15 predictions=[{\"Dashboard\": 2}, {\"Threads\": 3}, ...]\`

4. **Tick decision summary** (TRACE, per-frame):
   \`tick_strategy.frame: tick=1234 active=\"Messages\" ticked=[\"Dashboard\",\"Threads\"] skipped=[\"Analytics\",\"Contacts\",...]\`
   Note: TRACE level only — this fires every frame and would be overwhelming at DEBUG.

5. **Decay event** (DEBUG):
   \`tick_strategy.decay: factor=0.85 entries_before=45 entries_after=42 pruned=3\`

6. **Persistence save** (INFO):
   \`tick_strategy.save: path=\"/home/user/.mcp_agent_mail/tick_transitions.json\" transitions=247\`

7. **Persistence load** (INFO):
   \`tick_strategy.load: path=\"...\" loaded_transitions=189 confidence=0.87\`

8. **Cold start detected** (INFO):
   \`tick_strategy.cold_start: screen=\"Analytics\" observations=3 min_required=20 using_fallback=true\`

### Implementation

Use the \`tracing\` crate (already a dependency) with structured fields:
\`\`\`rust
use tracing::{debug, info, trace, instrument};

impl Predictive {
    fn refresh_cache(&mut self, active: &str) {
        let predictions = self.predictor.predict(active);
        debug!(
            strategy = self.name(),
            active_screen = active,
            num_screens = predictions.len(),
            "tick strategy cache refreshed"
        );
        for p in &predictions {
            trace!(screen = %p.screen, divisor = p.suggested_divisor, prob = %p.probability, "screen divisor");
        }
        // ... update cache
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Log Level Policy

| Level | What | When |
|-------|------|------|
| INFO | Strategy init, persist save/load, cold start | Startup, periodic |
| DEBUG | Transitions, cache refresh, decay | Per-transition, periodic |
| TRACE | Per-frame decisions, per-screen divisors | Every frame (high volume) |

### Testing

- Tracing events fire at correct levels
- Structured fields contain expected values
- No tracing at INFO level on every frame (would be too noisy)
- Cold start message appears when confidence < threshold

### Dependencies

- Depends on C.1 (TickStrategy trait — name() and debug_stats() methods)
- Depends on C.5 (Predictive strategy — tracing integration)
- Should be implemented alongside C.5, not after","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:02.386564213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:02.386564213Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["observability","perf","tick-strategy","tracing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:02.386564213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1nl75.8.2","title":"H.2: Debug stats surface for runtime introspection","description":"## H.2: Debug Stats Surface for Runtime Introspection

### What To Build

A way for apps to query the current state of the tick strategy at runtime. This enables debug screens, inspector overlays, and \`am robot\` commands to show tick strategy behavior.

### API

The TickStrategy trait already has \`debug_stats() -> Vec<(String, String)>\` (from C.1). This task implements it for each strategy and adds a query path from the Program.

### Per-Strategy Stats

**ActiveOnly:**
\`\`\`
[("strategy", "ActiveOnly"), ("background_ticks", "0")]
\`\`\`

**Uniform:**
\`\`\`
[("strategy", "Uniform"), ("divisor", "5"), ("effective_fps_bg", "2.0")]
\`\`\`

**Predictive:**
\`\`\`
[("strategy", "Predictive"),
 ("total_transitions", "247"),
 ("confidence", "0.87"),
 ("active_screen", "Messages"),
 ("top_prediction", "Dashboard:0.45/div=2"),
 ("cold_start_screens", "ArchiveBrowser,Contacts"),
 ("persisted_path", "/home/user/.mcp_agent_mail/tick_transitions.json"),
 ("last_save", "2026-02-24T03:15:00Z"),
 ("decay_factor", "0.85"),
 ("decay_next_at", "500")]
\`\`\`

### Query Path

\`\`\`rust
impl Program<M> {
    pub fn tick_strategy_stats(&self) -> Vec<(String, String)> {
        self.tick_strategy.as_ref()
            .map(|s| s.debug_stats())
            .unwrap_or_default()
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Integration with mcp-agent-mail

The \`am\` TUI can show tick strategy stats in:
- The SystemHealth screen (existing)
- The debug inspector overlay (existing, toggled by tui_debug)
- \`am robot health\` command output

### Testing

- Each strategy returns correct stats
- Stats update after transitions and decay
- Empty/default stats for no-strategy Program

### Dependencies

- Depends on C.1 (debug_stats method on trait)
- Depends on C.2-C.5 (each strategy implements debug_stats)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:17.533451155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:17.533451155Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["observability","perf","tick-strategy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:17.533451155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Cold start message appears when confidence < threshold

- Depends on C.1 (TickStrategy trait — name() and debug_stats() methods)
- Depends on C.5 (Predictive strategy — tracing integration)
- Should be implemented alongside C.5, not after","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:02.386564213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T03:20:32.975592813Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["observability","perf","tick-strategy","tracing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:20:31.468941986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:20:32.975518254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:02.386564213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1nl75.8.3","title":"H.3: Environment variable configuration for mcp-agent-mail tick strategy","description":"## H.3: Environment Variable Configuration for Tick Strategy

### What To Build

Add env vars to mcp-agent-mail-core/src/config.rs so operators can configure the tick strategy without code changes. Consistent with the project's 100+ env var configuration pattern.

### New Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| \`AM_TUI_TICK_STRATEGY\` | \`predictive\` | Strategy: \`active_only\`, \`uniform\`, \`adjacent\`, \`predictive\` |
| \`AM_TUI_TICK_DIVISOR\` | \`5\` | Divisor for Uniform strategy; fallback divisor for Predictive |
| \`AM_TUI_TICK_PERSIST\` | \`true\` | Enable transition data persistence across sessions |
| \`AM_TUI_TICK_MIN_OBSERVATIONS\` | \`20\` | Minimum transitions before Predictive predictions are trusted |
| \`AM_TUI_TICK_DECAY_FACTOR\` | \`0.85\` | Temporal decay factor (0.0-1.0) |

### Config Struct Addition

In Config:
\`\`\`rust
pub tui_tick_strategy: String,        // "predictive", "uniform", "adjacent", "active_only"
pub tui_tick_divisor: u64,            // fallback/uniform divisor
pub tui_tick_persist: bool,           // persist transition data
pub tui_tick_min_observations: u64,   // cold-start threshold
pub tui_tick_decay_factor: f64,       // temporal decay
\`\`\`

### Strategy Construction

In the TUI initialization code:
\`\`\`rust
let strategy = match config.tui_tick_strategy.as_str() {
    "active_only" => TickStrategyKind::ActiveOnly,
    "uniform" => TickStrategyKind::Uniform { divisor: config.tui_tick_divisor },
    "adjacent" => TickStrategyKind::ActivePlusAdjacent {
        screens: ALL_SCREEN_IDS.iter().map(|id| format!("{id:?}")).collect(),
        background_divisor: config.tui_tick_divisor,
    },
    "predictive" | _ => TickStrategyKind::Predictive {
        config: PredictiveConfig {
            fallback_divisor: config.tui_tick_divisor,
            min_observations: config.tui_tick_min_observations,
            decay_factor: config.tui_tick_decay_factor,
            ..PredictiveConfig::default()
        },
    },
};
\`\`\`

### Testing

- Default config selects Predictive strategy
- Each strategy string maps to correct TickStrategyKind
- Invalid strategy string falls back to Predictive
- Divisor=0 clamped to 1
- Decay factor outside 0.0-1.0 clamped
- Persist=false skips persistence path setup
- Test: env var override works (set AM_TUI_TICK_STRATEGY=uniform, verify Uniform selected)

### Dependencies

- Depends on C.1 (TickStrategyKind enum)
- Used by F.2 (strategy configuration in mcp-agent-mail)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:35.169691893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:35.169691893Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","mcp-agent-mail","perf","tick-strategy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:35.169691893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1nl75.9","title":"Track I: Comprehensive Test Suite","description":"## Track I: Comprehensive Test Suite

### Purpose

Dedicated track for all testing work. Tests are the PROOF that the system works. Each component gets unit tests inline, and there are integration tests and E2E scripts that exercise the full lifecycle with detailed logging.

### Testing Philosophy

- Every public function has at least one test
- Every edge case mentioned in a bead description becomes a test case
- Tests must include detailed assertions with human-readable failure messages
- E2E tests log every step so failures are immediately diagnosable
- Property tests verify mathematical invariants

### Subtasks

- I.1: Unit tests for TransitionCounter (A.1)
- I.2: Unit tests for MarkovPredictor (B.1)
- I.3: Unit tests for TickAllocation (B.2)
- I.4: Unit tests for each TickStrategy (C.2-C.6)
- I.5: Unit tests for persistence round-trip (E.1)
- I.6: Integration test: full lifecycle with mock model
- I.7: E2E test script for mcp-agent-mail tick strategy
- I.8: Property tests for Markov chain invariants","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:46.854632049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:46.854632049Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["perf","testing","tick-strategy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:17:46.854632049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1nl75.9.1","title":"I.1: Unit tests for TransitionCounter","description":"## I.1: Unit Tests for TransitionCounter

### Test Cases (minimum 15 tests)

**Basic recording:**
1. record() increments count for (from, to) pair
2. record() same pair multiple times: count grows correctly
3. count() for unrecorded pair returns 0
4. total_from() returns sum of all outgoing from a screen
5. total() returns sum of all transitions

**Probability:**
6. probability() with one transition: returns 1.0 (before smoothing)
7. probability() with multiple targets: sums to 1.0 (within epsilon)
8. probability() with Laplace smoothing: unseen transitions get small non-zero value
9. probability() for unknown screen: returns uniform (via smoothing)
10. all_targets_ranked(): sorted descending by probability

**Decay:**
11. decay(0.5) halves all counts
12. decay(1.0) is identity (no change)
13. decay(0.0) zeroes all counts
14. decay prunes entries below threshold
15. total_from cache consistent after decay

**Merge:**
16. merge() combines two counters additively
17. merge() with disjoint screens: union of all entries
18. merge() is commutative: merge(A,B) == merge(B,A) (verify counts)
19. merge() empty counter: identity

**Edge cases:**
20. screen_ids() on empty counter: empty set
21. screen_ids() returns all seen screens
22. record() with same from and to (self-loop): counted correctly

### Logging in Tests

Each test should use \`eprintln!\` for key intermediate values:
\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn probability_sums_to_one() {
    let mut counter = TransitionCounter::new();
    counter.record("A", "B");
    counter.record("A", "C");
    counter.record("A", "B");
    let targets = counter.all_targets_ranked("A");
    let sum: f64 = targets.iter().map(|(_, p)| p).sum();
    eprintln!("targets: {targets:?}, sum: {sum}");
    assert!((sum - 1.0).abs() < 1e-10, "probabilities must sum to 1.0, got {sum}");
}
\`\`\`

### Test Cases (minimum 8 tests)

1. Save empty counter → load → empty counter
2. Save counter with data → load → identical counts
3. Save → load → save → files are byte-identical
4. Missing file → load returns empty (no error)
5. Corrupted JSON → load returns error

7. Atomic write: temp file cleaned up on success
8. Large counter (1000+ entries): round-trip is correct
9. Counter with special characters in screen IDs: round-trip works
10. File permissions: read-only file → save returns error (not panic)

 {"id":"bd-1nl75.9.6","title":"I.6: Integration test — full lifecycle with MockMultiScreenModel","description":"## I.6: Integration Test — Full Lifecycle with MockMultiScreenModel

### What To Build

A comprehensive integration test that exercises the entire tick strategy system end-to-end using a mock multi-screen model. This is the definitive proof that all components work together.

### MockMultiScreenModel

\`\`\`rust
struct MockMultiScreenModel {
    screens: Vec<String>,
    active: usize,
    tick_counts: HashMap<String, u64>,  // per-screen tick counter
    total_ticks: u64,
}

impl Model for MockMultiScreenModel {
    type Message = MockMsg;
    fn update(&mut self, msg: MockMsg) -> Cmd<MockMsg> { /* ... */ }
    fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect) { /* minimal */ }
    fn as_screen_tick_dispatch(&mut self) -> Option<&mut dyn ScreenTickDispatch> {
        Some(self)
    }
}

impl ScreenTickDispatch for MockMultiScreenModel {
    fn screen_ids(&self) -> Vec<&str> { self.screens.iter().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect() }
    fn active_screen_id(&self) -> &str { &self.screens[self.active] }
    fn tick_screen(&mut self, screen_id: &str, _tick: u64) {
        *self.tick_counts.entry(screen_id.to_string()).or_default() += 1;
        self.total_ticks += 1;
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Test Scenarios (minimum 8 tests)

1. **No strategy (baseline)**: All screens tick every frame. Total = N × frames.

2. **ActiveOnly**: Only active screen ticks. Total = frames.

3. **Uniform(5)**: Active every frame + inactive every 5th. Verify exact counts.

4. **Predictive cold start**: Similar to Uniform (no data → fallback).

5. **Predictive warm**: Record 100 transitions A→B (80%), A→C (15%), A→D (5%). Run 200 frames. Verify B ticks most, D ticks least.

6. **Screen switch force-tick**: Switch from A to B. Verify B gets immediate tick. Previous active A stops getting per-frame ticks.

7. **Screen switch updates predictions**: After switching A→B 50 times, predictions from A should heavily favor B.

8. **Persistence round-trip**: Create Predictive with transitions. Save. Create new Predictive loading saved data. Verify predictions match.

### Logging

Each test logs tick counts per screen at end:
\`\`\`rust
eprintln!("=== Tick distribution after {frames} frames ===");
for (screen, count) in &model.tick_counts {
    eprintln!("  {screen}: {count} ticks ({:.1}%)", *count as f64 / model.total_ticks as f64 * 100.0);
}
\`\`\`

### Dependencies

- Depends on C.5 (Predictive strategy)
- Depends on D.2 (runtime tick dispatch)
- Depends on D.4 (ScreenTickDispatch trait)
- Depends on E.1 (persistence for round-trip test)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:19:08.288413918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T03:19:08.288413918Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["integration","perf","testing","tick-strategy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:19:08.288413918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1nl75.9.7","title":"I.7: E2E test script for mcp-agent-mail tick strategy","description":"## I.7: E2E Test Script for mcp-agent-mail Tick Strategy

### What To Build

A bash E2E test script \`tests/e2e/test_tick_strategy.sh\` that validates tick strategy behavior in the actual \`am\` binary. This proves the framework integration works in production, not just in unit tests.

### Script Structure

\`\`\`bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR=\"\$(cd \"\$(dirname \"\${BASH_SOURCE[0]}\")\" && pwd)\"
source \"\${SCRIPT_DIR}/e2e_lib.sh\"

section \"Tick Strategy E2E Tests\"

# --- Test 1: Default strategy is Predictive ---
test_default_strategy() {
    log \"Testing default tick strategy selection\"
    local output
    output=\$(AM_TUI_TICK_STRATEGY=predictive am robot health --format json 2>&1)
    assert_contains \"\$output\" \"tick_strategy\" \"health output should include tick strategy info\"
    pass \"default strategy is Predictive\"
}

# --- Test 2: Uniform strategy via env var ---
test_uniform_strategy() {
    log \"Testing AM_TUI_TICK_STRATEGY=uniform\"
    local output
    output=\$(AM_TUI_TICK_STRATEGY=uniform AM_TUI_TICK_DIVISOR=10 am robot health --format json 2>&1)
    assert_contains \"\$output\" \"Uniform\" \"should report Uniform strategy\"
    pass \"uniform strategy configurable via env var\"
}

# --- Test 3: ActiveOnly strategy ---
test_active_only_strategy() {
    log \"Testing AM_TUI_TICK_STRATEGY=active_only\"
    local output
    output=\$(AM_TUI_TICK_STRATEGY=active_only am robot health --format json 2>&1)
    assert_contains \"\$output\" \"ActiveOnly\" \"should report ActiveOnly strategy\"
    pass \"active_only strategy works\"
}

# --- Test 4: Persistence file creation ---
test_persistence_creates_file() {
    log \"Testing tick transition persistence\"
    local tmp_storage
    tmp_storage=\$(mktemp -d)
    # Start am briefly to generate some transitions
    timeout 5 am --storage-root \"\$tmp_storage\" serve-http --no-auth 2>/dev/null || true
    if [[ -f \"\$tmp_storage/tick_transitions.json\" ]]; then
        local content
        content=\$(cat \"\$tmp_storage/tick_transitions.json\")
        assert_contains \"\$content\" \"version\" \"persistence file should have version\"
        pass \"persistence file created\"
    else
        skip \"persistence file not created (server may not have run long enough)\"
    fi
    rm -rf \"\$tmp_storage\"
}

# --- Test 5: Invalid strategy falls back gracefully ---
test_invalid_strategy_fallback() {
    log \"Testing invalid strategy name falls back to Predictive\"
    local output
    output=\$(AM_TUI_TICK_STRATEGY=nonsense am robot health --format json 2>&1)
    assert_contains \"\$output\" \"Predictive\" \"invalid strategy should fall back to Predictive\"
    pass \"invalid strategy falls back gracefully\"
}

# --- Test 6: Divisor clamping ---
test_divisor_clamping() {
    log \"Testing AM_TUI_TICK_DIVISOR=0 clamps to 1\"
    local output
    output=\$(AM_TUI_TICK_STRATEGY=uniform AM_TUI_TICK_DIVISOR=0 am robot health --format json 2>&1)
    # Should not crash, divisor should be 1
    assert_exit_code 0 \"am with divisor=0 should not crash\"
    pass \"divisor=0 clamped to 1\"
}

run_tests test_default_strategy test_uniform_strategy test_active_only_strategy \\
          test_persistence_creates_file test_invalid_strategy_fallback test_divisor_clamping

summary
\`\`\`

### Logging

Every test function starts with \`log \"Testing ...\"\` and ends with \`pass\` or \`fail\`.
The e2e_lib.sh provides section/log/assert_*/pass/fail/summary helpers already used by other E2E suites.

### Expected Assertions: 6+

### Dependencies

- Depends on F.2 (mcp-agent-mail migration to framework strategy)
- Depends on H.3 (env var configuration)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:19:31.539797552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T03:19:31.539797552Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","mcp-agent-mail","perf","testing","tick-strategy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.9.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:19:31.539797552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1nl75.9.8","title":"I.8: Property tests — Markov chain mathematical invariants","description":"## I.8: Property Tests — Markov Chain Mathematical Invariants

### What To Build

proptest-based property tests verifying mathematical invariants hold for all inputs. These catch edge cases that hand-written tests miss.

### Invariants

1. **Probability normalization**: sum of all outgoing probabilities from any screen == 1.0 (±epsilon)
2. **Monotonicity**: count(A→B) > count(A→C) implies probability(A→B) >= probability(A→C)
3. **Divisor monotonicity**: higher probability → lower or equal divisor
4. **Decay preserves relative order**: if count(A→B) > count(A→C) before decay, same after
5. **Merge commutativity**: merge(X,Y) counts == merge(Y,X) counts
6. **Merge associativity**: merge(merge(X,Y),Z) == merge(X,merge(Y,Z))
7. **Round-trip serialization**: load(save(counter)) == counter
8. **Confidence bounds**: 0.0 <= confidence(screen) <= 1.0
9. **Divisor bounds**: min_divisor <= divisor_for(p) <= max_divisor for 0<=p<=1
10. **Decay monotonic**: for 0 < f1 < f2 < 1, decay(f1) produces smaller counts than decay(f2)

### proptest Strategy

\`\`\`rust
use proptest::prelude::*;

fn arb_screen_id() -> impl Strategy<Value = String> {
    prop::string::string_regex(\"[A-Z][a-z]{2,10}\").unwrap()
}

fn arb_transitions(max_n: usize) -> impl Strategy<Value = Vec<(String, String)>> {
    prop::collection::vec((arb_screen_id(), arb_screen_id()), 1..max_n)
}

proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn probabilities_sum_to_one(transitions in arb_transitions(100)) {
        let mut counter = TransitionCounter::new();
        for (from, to) in &transitions {
            counter.record(from.clone(), to.clone());
        }
        for from in counter.screen_ids() {
            let targets = counter.all_targets_ranked(&from);
            let sum: f64 = targets.iter().map(|(_, p)| p).sum();
            prop_assert!((sum - 1.0).abs() < 1e-9, \"sum={sum} for screen {from}\");
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Dependencies

- Depends on A.1, B.1, B.2, E.1 (implementations to test)
- G.3 (previously created) can be merged into this or superseded by it","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:19:58.718195262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T03:19:58.718195262Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["perf","testing","tick-strategy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nl75.9.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1nl75.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T03:19:58.718195262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1nn0","title":"Add recovery mechanism with configurable attempts","description":"# Add recovery mechanism with configurable attempts

## Background
Part of Widget Error Boundaries (bd-1euu). After a widget fails, we should try to recover when conditions change (e.g., model updates).

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Configuration for error boundary behavior
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct ErrorBoundaryConfig {
    /// Maximum recovery attempts before giving up
    pub max_recovery_attempts: u32,
    
    /// Delay between recovery attempts (in frames)
    pub recovery_delay_frames: u32,
    
    /// Whether to log errors
    pub log_errors: bool,
    
    /// Custom fallback widget factory
    pub fallback_factory: Option<Box<dyn Fn(&CapturedError) -> Box<dyn Widget>>>,
    
    /// Whether to try recovery on model update
    pub recover_on_model_update: bool,
}

impl Default for ErrorBoundaryConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            max_recovery_attempts: 3,
            recovery_delay_frames: 5,
            log_errors: true,
            fallback_factory: None,
            recover_on_model_update: true,
        }
    }
}

impl<W: Widget> ErrorBoundary<W> {
    /// Attempt to recover from error state
    /// Call this when model changes or after delay
    pub fn try_recover(&mut self) -> bool {
        match &mut self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error) => {
                if self.config.max_recovery_attempts > 0 {
                    tracing::info!(
                        widget = self.name,
                        attempt = 1,
                        "Starting recovery attempt"
                    );
                    self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering {
                        attempts: 1,
                        last_error: error.clone(),
                    };
                    true
                } else {
                    false
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { attempts, last_error } => {
                if *attempts < self.config.max_recovery_attempts {
                    *attempts += 1;
                    tracing::info!(
                        widget = self.name,
                        attempt = *attempts,
                        max = self.config.max_recovery_attempts,
                        "Continuing recovery attempt"
                    );
                    true
                } else {
                    // Give up permanently
                    self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(last_error.clone());
                    tracing::warn!(
                        widget = self.name,
                        "Recovery failed after max attempts"
                    );
                    false
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => true,
        }
    }
    
    /// Reset to healthy state (force recovery)
    pub fn reset(&mut self) {
        self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy;
        tracing::info!(widget = self.name, "Error boundary reset");
    }
    
    /// Signal that model has updated (may trigger recovery)
    pub fn on_model_update(&mut self) {
        if self.config.recover_on_model_update && self.is_failed() {
            self.try_recover();
        }
    }
    
    /// Get recovery status
    pub fn recovery_status(&self) -> RecoveryStatus {
        match &self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => RecoveryStatus::Healthy,
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(_) => RecoveryStatus::Failed,
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { attempts, .. } => {
                RecoveryStatus::Recovering {
                    attempts: *attempts,
                    max_attempts: self.config.max_recovery_attempts,
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

/// Recovery status for external monitoring
#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum RecoveryStatus {
    Healthy,
    Failed,
    Recovering { attempts: u32, max_attempts: u32 },
}
```

## Recovery Triggers
1. **Manual**: Call try_recover() explicitly
2. **Model update**: Automatic when config.recover_on_model_update
3. **Force reset**: Call reset() to immediately clear error

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ErrorBoundaryConfig with all options
- [ ] try_recover() increments attempts
- [ ] Max attempts enforced
- [ ] reset() forces healthy state
- [ ] on_model_update() triggers recovery
- [ ] RecoveryStatus for monitoring","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:18.791599230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:29:50.166964103Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:29:50.166938815Z","close_reason":"Recovery mechanism already implemented in ErrorBoundaryState with try_recover(max_attempts), Recovering state, attempts counter, and configurable max_recovery_attempts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nn0","depends_on_id":"bd-1fpo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
